explanation: The Andromeda Galaxy is the nearest large spiral to our own, its dust lanes sweeping in long curves against a field of older golden stars. The structure makes for striking reference when sketching cosmic linework.
title: M31: The Andromeda Galaxy

explanation: The Pleiades star cluster glows blue inside a haze of reflection nebulosity. The soft scatter of light around each star reads almost like ink bleeding into wet paper.
title: The Pleiades Star Cluster

explanation: The Orion Nebula is a vast stellar nursery, its folds of glowing gas lit from within by young stars. The interior shapes carry the kind of organic flow worth studying for ornamental flourishes.
title: M42: The Orion Nebula

explanation: The Horsehead Nebula stands as a dark silhouette against a backdrop of red emission gas. A clean negative-space form, the sort that translates well into a single bold mark.
title: The Horsehead Nebula

explanation: The Pillars of Creation rise in towering columns of cold gas and dust, sculpted by nearby stellar winds. The tapering verticals echo the rhythm of an ascending stroke.
title: The Pillars of Creation

explanation: The core of the Milky Way arches over a quiet horizon, dense with star clouds and dark dust lanes. The band of light gives a natural baseline curve to build a layout around.
title: The Milky Way Core Rising

explanation: A total lunar eclipse turns the Moon a deep copper red as it passes through Earth's shadow. The warm tone sits squarely in the earthy palette that suits this project.
title: A Total Lunar Eclipse

explanation: Saturn presents its rings near maximum tilt, the banded disk framed by thin concentric lines. The geometry is a quiet study in repetition and spacing.
title: Saturn and Its Rings

explanation: Jupiter shows its banded cloud belts and the long-lived Great Red Spot. The swirling boundary between belts is a lesson in how two flows can meet on a page.
title: Jupiter's Cloud Bands

explanation: A green aurora ripples across a clear northern sky, its curtains folding and stretching overhead. The drape of the light is a good model for ribbon and banner forms.
title: Aurora Over a Northern Sky

explanation: Comet C/2023 A3 trails a long ion tail against a dark winter sky. A single sweeping gesture, exactly the kind of line a flourish wants to imitate.
title: A Comet's Long Tail

explanation: The Lagoon Nebula glows in pink hydrogen light, threaded with dark lanes and bright young stars. Soft edges and warm color, useful for testing a watercolor underlay.
title: M8: The Lagoon Nebula

explanation: The Rosette Nebula forms a near-circular bloom of red gas around a central cluster. The radial symmetry suggests a monogram set inside a wreath.
title: The Rosette Nebula

explanation: The Whirlpool Galaxy and its smaller companion lock into a graceful spiral pairing. Two forms in balanced tension, a composition worth borrowing for a paired monogram.
title: M51: The Whirlpool Galaxy

explanation: The Crab Nebula spreads its filaments outward from a long-dead supernova. The tangled threads read like overlapping ribbons caught mid-motion.
title: M1: The Crab Nebula

explanation: A thin crescent Moon hangs near Venus low in the evening sky. Two simple shapes and a lot of negative space, a reminder that restraint reads as elegance.
title: Crescent Moon and Venus

explanation: The Eagle Nebula glows around a young open cluster, its dust pillars rising into the light. Layered depth here is a good study for stacking foil over a printed ground.
title: M16: The Eagle Nebula

explanation: Star trails arc in concentric circles above a still landscape during a long exposure. The nested curves map almost directly onto a curved baseline exercise.
title: Star Trails Over a Quiet Field

explanation: The Triangulum Galaxy unwinds in loose blue spiral arms dotted with pink star-forming regions. An airy, open structure, useful when a layout needs room to breathe.
title: M33: The Triangulum Galaxy

explanation: The Helix Nebula stares out as a vast ring of glowing gas, often called the Eye of God. A perfect circular frame, ready to hold a couple's initials at its center.
title: The Helix Nebula
